# `freebox_vpn_server` (Resource)

Manages the OpenVPN server configuration on the Freebox. This is a singleton resource: only one OpenVPN server exists per Freebox. Destroying this resource disables the VPN server.

## Example

```terraform
resource "freebox_vpn_server" "example" {
  enabled     = true
  server_port = 1194
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Optional

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the OpenVPN server is enabled
- `push_dhcp` (Boolean) Whether to push DHCP settings to clients
- `server_ip` (String) VPN subnet IP address (e.g. "10.8.0.0")
- `server_mask` (String) VPN subnet mask (e.g. "255.255.255.0")
- `server_port` (Number) UDP port the OpenVPN server listens on (default 1194)

### Read-Only

- `ca` (String, Sensitive) CA certificate in PEM format (read-only, set by Freebox)
- `id` (String) Fixed identifier for the singleton OpenVPN server resource

## Import

```sh
# The VPN server is a singleton resource; use "openvpn" as the import ID
terraform import "freebox_vpn_server.example" openvpn
```
